reasoning: 'at' means 'to compress', appears in compounds like 'mâai at' (compressed wood = line) and 'nám at lôm' (compressed water/air = carbonated drink)
'chîang mai' is Chiang Mai, a city in Northern Thailand
'dòm' means 'to boil', appears in 'kāa dòm' (boiling pot = kettle) and 'khai dòm kháeng' (boiled egg hard = hard-boiled egg)
'kāa dòm' means 'kettle' (pot for boiling), combining pot + boil
'kāa kâafāae' means 'coffee pot', where kâafāae = coffee
'khâao mǎn kai' is Hainan chicken rice, a Southeast Asian dish
'khai' means 'egg', basic component word
'khai dòm kháeng' means 'hard-boiled egg' (egg boil hard)
'khai kai' means 'chicken egg' (egg chicken)
'lôm nǎao' means 'cold air' (air cold)
'mâai at' means 'line' (wood/straight compressed)
'mai' means 'line of trees' or 'wood', the tree/wood version of mai
'mǎn' means 'oil/grease', appears in 'nám mǎn' (gasoline) and 'khâao mǎn kai' (chicken fat rice)
'nǎeo' means 'new' by process of elimination, though it also relates to line/strip in compounds
'nǎeo mâai' means 'plywood' (strips/layers of wood)
'nám' means 'water', appears in multiple compounds
'nám at lôm' means 'carbonated drink' (water compressed air)
'nám kháeng' means 'ice' (water hard)
'râakhǎa mai' means 'new price' (price new), where mai = new (different from wood mai)
'râakhǎa nám mǎn' means 'the price of gasoline' (price water-oil)
'khâao' appears in 'khâao mǎn kai' (Hainan chicken rice), so khâao = rice
'kāa' appears in 'kāa dòm' (kettle) and 'kāa kâafāae' (coffee pot), so kāa = pot
'chîang' appears in 'chîang mai' (Chiang Mai city), so chîang = Chiang (city name component)
Beautiful line would be 'mâai suay', where mâai relates to line (from mâai at = line) and suay = beautiful (mentioned in problem description)
